verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est cum intraret Jesus in domum cujusdam principis pharisæorum sabbato manducare panem, et ipsi observabant eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ἐν τῷ ἐλθεῖν αὐτὸν εἰς οἶκόν τινος τῶν ἀρχόντων τῶν φαρισαίων σαββάτῳ φαγεῖν ἄρτον καὶ αὐτοὶ ἦσαν παρατηρούμενοι αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, as he went into the house of one of the chief Pharisees to eat bread on the sabbath day, that they watched him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce homo quidam hydropicus erat ante illum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού, ἄνθρωπός τις ἦν ὑδρωπικὸς ἔμπροσθεν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And, behold, there was a certain man before him which had the dropsy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens Jesus dixit ad legisperitos et pharisæos, dicens: Si licet sabbato curare?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν πρὸς τοὺς νομικοὺς καὶ φαρισαίους λέγων, ἐι ἕξεστιν τῷ σαββάτῳ θεραπεύειν
verses.king_james: And Jesus answering spake unto the lawyers and Pharisees, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath day?

verses.latin_vulgate: At illi tacuerunt. Ipse vero apprehensum sanavit eum, ac dimisit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ ἡσύχασαν καὶ ἐπιλαβόμενος ἰάσατο αὐτὸν καὶ ἀπέλυσεν
verses.king_james: And they held their peace. And he took *him*, and healed him, and let him go;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens ad illos dixit: Cujus vestrum asinus, aut bos in puteum cadet, et non continuo extrahet illum die sabbati?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς πρὸς αὐτοὺς εἶπεν τίνος ὑμῶν ὄνος ἢ βοῦς εἰς φρέαρ ἐμπεσεῖται, καὶ οὐκ εὐθέως ἀνασπάσει αὐτὸν ἐν τὴ ἡμέρᾳ τοῦ σαββάτου
verses.king_james: And answered them, saying, Which of you shall have an ass or an ox fallen into a pit, and will not straightway pull him out on the sabbath day?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non poterant ad hæc respondere illi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὐκ ἴσχυσαν ἀνταποκριθῆναι αὐτῷ πρὸς ταῦτα
verses.king_james: And they could not answer him again to these things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebat autem et ad invitatos parabolam, intendens quomodo primos accubitus eligerent, dicens ad illos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕλεγεν δὲ πρὸς τοὺς κεκλημένους παραβολήν ἐπέχων πῶς τὰς πρωτοκλισίας ἐξελέγοντο λέγων πρὸς αὐτούς
verses.king_james: And he put forth a parable to those which were bidden, when he marked how they chose out the chief rooms; saying unto them,

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum invitatus fueris ad nuptias, non discumbas in primo loco, ne forte honoratior te sit invitatus ab illo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν κληθῇς ὑπό τινος εἰς γάμους μὴ κατακλιθῇς εἰς τὴν πρωτοκλισίαν μήποτε ἐντιμότερός σου ᾖ κεκλημένος ὑπ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: When thou art bidden of any *man* to a wedding, sit not down in the highest room; lest a more honourable man than thou be bidden of him;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et veniens is, qui te et illum vocavit, dicat tibi: Da huic locum: et tunc incipias cum rubore novissimum locum tenere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐλθὼν ὁ σὲ καὶ αὐτὸν καλέσας ἐρεῖ σοι δὸς τούτῳ τόπον καὶ τότε ἄρξῃ μετ᾽ αἰσχύνης τὸν ἔσχατον τόπον κατέχειν
verses.king_james: And he that bade thee and him come and say to thee, Give this man place; and thou begin with shame to take the lowest room.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed cum vocatus fueris, vade, recumbe in novissimo loco: ut, cum venerit qui te invitavit, dicat tibi: Amice, ascende superius. Tunc erit tibi gloria coram simul discumbentibus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ὅταν κληθῇς πορευθεὶς ἀνάπεσον εἰς τὸν ἔσχατον τόπον ἵνα ὅταν ἔλθῃ ὁ κεκληκώς σε εἴπῃ σοι φίλε προσανάβηθι ἀνώτερον τότε ἔσται σοι δόξα ἐνώπιον τῶν συνανακειμένων σοι
verses.king_james: But when thou art bidden, go and sit down in the lowest room; that when he that bade thee cometh, he may say unto thee, Friend, go up higher: then shalt thou have worship in the presence of them that sit at meat with thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia omnis, qui se exaltat, humiliabitur: et qui se humiliat, exaltabitur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι πᾶς ὁ ὑψῶν ἑαυτὸν ταπεινωθήσεται καὶ ὁ ταπεινῶν ἑαυτὸν ὑψωθήσεται
verses.king_james: For whosoever exalteth himself shall be abased; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebat autem et ei, qui invitaverat: Cum facis prandium, aut cœnam, noli vocare amicos tuos, neque fratres tuos, neque cognatos, neque vicinos divites: ne forte te et ipsi reinvitent, et fiat tibi retributio;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕλεγεν δὲ καὶ τῷ κεκληκότι αὐτόν ὅταν ποιῇς ἄριστον ἢ δεῖπνον μὴ φώνει τοὺς φίλους σου μηδὲ τοὺς ἀδελφούς σου μηδὲ τοὺς συγγενεῖς σου μηδὲ γείτονας πλουσίους μήποτε καὶ αὐτοὶ σε ἀντικαλέσωσίν καὶ γένηται σοι ἀνταπόδομά
verses.king_james: Then said he also to him that bade him, When thou makest a dinner or a supper, call not thy friends, nor thy brethren, neither thy kinsmen, nor *thy* rich neighbours; lest they also bid thee again, and a recompence be made thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: sed cum facis convivium, voca pauperes, debiles, claudos, et cæcos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ὅταν ποιῇς δοχὴν κάλει πτωχούς ἀναπήρους, χωλούς τυφλούς
verses.king_james: But when thou makest a feast, call the poor, the maimed, the lame, the blind:

verses.latin_vulgate: et beatus eris, quia non habent retribuere tibi: retribuetur enim tibi in resurrectione justorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μακάριος ἔσῃ ὅτι οὐκ ἔχουσιν ἀνταποδοῦναί σοι ἀνταποδοθήσεται γάρ σοι ἐν τῇ ἀναστάσει τῶν δικαίων
verses.king_james: And thou shalt be blessed; for they cannot recompense thee: for thou shalt be recompensed at the resurrection of the just.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc cum audisset quidam de simul discumbentibus, dixit illi: Beatus qui manducabit panem in regno Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσας δέ τις τῶν συνανακειμένων ταῦτα εἶπεν αὐτῷ μακάριος ὃς φάγεται ἄρτον ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And when one of them that sat at meat with him heard these things, he said unto him, Blessed *is* he that shall eat bread in the kingdom of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ipse dixit ei: Homo quidam fecit cœnam magnam, et vocavit multos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἄνθρωπός τις ἐποίησεν δεῖπνον μέγα καὶ ἐκάλεσεν πολλούς
verses.king_james: Then said he unto him, A certain man made a great supper, and bade many:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et misit servum suum hora cœnæ dicere invitatis ut venirent, quia jam parata sunt omnia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπέστειλεν τὸν δοῦλον αὐτοῦ τῇ ὥρᾳ τοῦ δείπνου εἰπεῖν τοῖς κεκλημένοις ἕρχεσθε ὅτι ἤδη ἕτοιμά ἐστιν πάντα
verses.king_james: And sent his servant at supper time to say to them that were bidden, Come; for all things are now ready.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cœperunt simul omnes excusare. Primus dixit ei: Villam emi, et necesse habeo exire, et videre illam: rogo te, habe me excusatum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἤρξαντο ἀπὸ μιᾶς παραιτεῖσθαι πάντες ὁ πρῶτος εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἀγρὸν ἠγόρασα καὶ ἔχω ἀνάγκην ἐξελθεῖν καὶ ἰδεῖν αὐτόν ἐρωτῶ σε ἔχε με παρῃτημένον
verses.king_james: And they all with one *consent* began to make excuse. The first said unto him, I have bought a piece of ground, and I must needs go and see it: I pray thee have me excused.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et alter dixit: Juga boum emi quinque, et eo probare illa: rogo te, habe me excusatum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἕτερος εἶπεν ξεύγη βοῶν ἠγόρασα πέντε καὶ πορεύομαι δοκιμάσαι αὐτά ἐρωτῶ σε ἔχε με παρῃτημένον
verses.king_james: And another said, I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I go to prove them: I pray thee have me excused.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et alius dixit: Uxorem duxi, et ideo non possum venire.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἕτερος εἶπεν γυναῖκα ἔγημα καὶ διὰ τοῦτο οὐ δύναμαι ἐλθεῖν
verses.king_james: And another said, I have married a wife, and therefore I cannot come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et reversus servus nuntiavit hæc domino suo. Tunc iratus paterfamilias, dixit servo suo: Exi cito in plateas et vicos civitatis: et pauperes, ac debiles, et cæcos, et claudos introduc huc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παραγενόμενος ὁ δοῦλος ἐκεῖνος ἀπήγγειλεν τῷ κυρίῳ αὐτοῦ ταῦτα τότε ὀργισθεὶς ὁ οἰκοδεσπότης εἶπεν τῷ δούλῳ αὐτοῦ ἕξελθε ταχέως εἰς τὰς πλατείας καὶ ῥύμας τῆς πόλεως καὶ τοὺς πτωχοὺς καὶ ἀναπήρους καὶ χωλοὺς καὶ τυφλοὺς εἰσάγαγε ὧδε
verses.king_james: So that servant came, and shewed his lord these things. Then the master of the house being angry said to his servant, Go out quickly into the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in hither the poor, and the maimed, and the halt, and the blind.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait servus: Domine, factum est ut imperasti, et adhuc locus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν ὁ δοῦλος κύριε γέγονεν ὡς ἐπέταξας καὶ ἔτι τόπος ἐστίν
verses.king_james: And the servant said, Lord, it is done as thou hast commanded, and yet there is room.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait dominus servo: Exi in vias, et sæpes: et compelle intrare, ut impleatur domus mea.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν ὁ κύριος πρὸς τὸν δοῦλον ἕξελθε εἰς τὰς ὁδοὺς καὶ φραγμοὺς καὶ ἀνάγκασον εἰσελθεῖν ἵνα γεμισθῇ ὁ οἶκος μου
verses.king_james: And the lord said unto the servant, Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel *them* to come in, that my house may be filled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico autem vobis quod nemo virorum illorum qui vocati sunt, gustabit cœnam meam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω γὰρ ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐδεὶς τῶν ἀνδρῶν ἐκείνων τῶν κεκλημένων γεύσεταί μου τοῦ δείπνου
verses.king_james: For I say unto you, That none of those men which were bidden shall taste of my supper.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ibant autem turbæ multæ cum eo: et conversus dixit ad illos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνεπορεύοντο δὲ αὐτῷ ὄχλοι πολλοί καὶ στραφεὶς εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτούς
verses.king_james: And there went great multitudes with him: and he turned, and said unto them,

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis venit ad me, et non odit patrem suum, et matrem, et uxorem, et filios, et fratres, et sorores, adhuc autem et animam suam, non potest meus esse discipulus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴ τις ἔρχεται πρός με καὶ οὐ μισεῖ τὸν πατέρα ἑαυτοῦ καὶ τὴν μητέρα καὶ τὴν γυναῖκα καὶ τὰ τέκνα καὶ τοὺς ἀδελφοὺς καὶ τὰς ἀδελφάς ἔτι δέ καὶ τὴν ἑαυτοῦ ψυχὴν οὐ δύναται μου μαθητής εἶναί
verses.king_james: If any *man* come to me, and hate not his father, and mother, and wife, and children, and brethren, and sisters, yea, and his own life also, he cannot be my disciple.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et qui non bajulat crucem suam, et venit post me, non potest meus esse discipulus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅστις οὐ βαστάζει τὸν σταυρὸν αὑτοῦ καὶ ἔρχεται ὀπίσω μου οὐ δύναται μου εἶναί μαθητής
verses.king_james: And whosoever doth not bear his cross, and come after me, cannot be my disciple.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis enim ex vobis volens turrim ædificare, non prius sedens computat sumptus, qui necessarii sunt, si habeat ad perficiendum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τίς γὰρ ἐξ ὑμῶν θέλων πύργον οἰκοδομῆσαι οὐχὶ πρῶτον καθίσας ψηφίζει τὴν δαπάνην εἰ ἔχει τὰ πρὸς ἀπαρτισμόν
verses.king_james: For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost, whether he have *sufficient* to finish *it*?

verses.latin_vulgate: ne, posteaquam posuerit fundamentum, et non potuerit perficere, omnes qui vident, incipiant illudere ei,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα μήποτε θέντος αὐτοῦ θεμέλιον καὶ μὴ ἰσχύοντος ἐκτελέσαι πάντες οἱ θεωροῦντες ἄρξωνται ἐμπαίζειν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: Lest haply, after he hath laid the foundation, and is not able to finish *it*, all that behold *it* begin to mock him,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicentes: Quia hic homo cœpit ædificare, et non potuit consummare?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγοντες ὅτι οὗτος ὁ ἄνθρωπος ἤρξατο οἰκοδομεῖν καὶ οὐκ ἴσχυσεν ἐκτελέσαι
verses.king_james: Saying, This man began to build, and was not able to finish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aut quis rex iturus committere bellum adversus alium regem, non sedens prius cogitat, si possit cum decem millibus occurrere ei, qui cum viginti millibus venit ad se?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ τίς βασιλεὺς πορευόμενος συμβαλεῖν ἑτέρῳ βασιλεῖ εἰς πόλεμον οὐχὶ καθίσας πρῶτον βουλεύεται εἰ δυνατός ἐστιν ἐν δέκα χιλιάσιν ἀπαντῆσαι τῷ μετὰ εἴκοσι χιλιάδων ἐρχομένῳ ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Or what king, going to make war against another king, sitteth not down first, and consulteth whether he be able with ten thousand to meet him that cometh against him with twenty thousand?

verses.latin_vulgate: Alioquin adhuc illo longe agente, legationem mittens rogat ea quæ pacis sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ μήγε, ἔτι αὐτοῦ πόῤῥω ὄντος πρεσβείαν ἀποστείλας ἐρωτᾷ τὰ πρὸς εἰρήνην
verses.king_james: Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sendeth an ambassage, and desireth conditions of peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic ergo omnis ex vobis, qui non renuntiat omnibus quæ possidet, non potest meus esse discipulus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως οὖν πᾶς ἐξ ὑμῶν ὃς οὐκ ἀποτάσσεται πᾶσιν τοῖς ἑαυτοῦ ὑπάρχουσιν οὐ δύναται μου εἶναί μαθητής
verses.king_james: So likewise, whosoever he be of you that forsaketh not all that he hath, he cannot be my disciple.

verses.latin_vulgate: Bonum est sal: si autem sal evanuerit, in quo condietur?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καλὸν τὸ ἅλας ἐὰν δὲ τὸ ἅλας μωρανθῇ ἐν τίνι ἀρτυθήσεται
verses.king_james: Salt *is* good: but if the salt have lost his savour, wherewith shall it be seasoned?

verses.latin_vulgate: Neque in terram, neque in sterquilinium utile est, sed foras mittetur. Qui habet aures audiendi, audiat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὔτε εἰς γῆν οὔτε εἰς κοπρίαν εὔθετόν ἐστιν ἔξω βάλλουσιν αὐτό ὁ ἔχων ὦτα ἀκούειν ἀκουέτω
verses.king_james: It is neither fit for the land, nor yet for the dunghill; *but* men cast it out. He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
